text: The surgeon introduces a trocar into the umbilicus and uses an aspiration needle to remove approximately fifteen liters of cystic fluid from a multiloculated cyst. The fluid fills the abdominal cavity, and the surgeon uses a fiber suction with high suction pressure to aspirate the spilled content while maintaining pneumoperitoneum with a high-capacity insufflator. Multiple cystic pockets are punctured and aspirated to create space for further surgical procedures.

The surgeon uses a vibrating jaw of the harmonic device to puncture another cyst, ensuring no remote injury due to its vibration mechanism. The fallopian tube is visible, and the surgeon manipulates it to assess the infundibulopelvic ligament. The destroyed ovary is noted, and the cystic wall is held to determine orientation. Suction irrigation is used to remove fluid from the septal compartments. Care is taken to avoid bowel adhesion, particularly near the sigmoidal colon and infundibulopelvic ligament.

The surgeon opens the thick outer ovarian cortex, which appears stretched due to the large cyst. A septum within the cyst is identified, and intramedial traction is applied to facilitate complete removal. The infundibulopelvic ligament is visible, with the sigmoid colon located below on the left side.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to carefully dissect around the infundulopelvic ligament on the left side, ensuring a safe distance from the ureter and sigmoid colon to avoid injury. The omentum is visible, and attention is given to the adhesions present in the area.

The surgeon performs a colpotomy in the posterior fornix, revealing the left ovary and fallopian tube, as well as the right ovary. A sponge is placed inside the abdomen, and claw forceps are used to hold and manually morcellate the cystic wall, pulling it through the vagina. Care is taken to avoid pulling the rectum and sigmoid colon. The multiloculated cyst septa are punctured and aspirated, and a drain is placed in the vagina to manage any fluid collection.

The surgeon carefully collapses and extracts multiple cysts, each punctured and drained to facilitate removal. The cysts are multiloculated with numerous septa, requiring careful suction and manipulation. Once extracted, thorough lavage is performed to clear any remaining fluid and debris from the surgical field.
